channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thousands gather outside the Vatican to pray for Pope Francis' health
item.description: Thousands of people gathered in St. Peter’s Square to pray for an ailing Pope Francis, expressing sorrow for his suffering, hope for his recovery and gratitude for his efforts to steer the Catholic Church in new directions.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell' Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anti-US sentiment bubbling up in the West Bank bolsters demand for a local Coke-alternative
item.description: The caramel brown liquid, red-and-white soda cans and splashy big font bear remarkable resemblance to an iconic American soft drink.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Palestinians struggle to restart their lives in the ruins of Gaza
item.description: Palestinians who returned to their homes in northern Gaza after the ceasefire began on Jan. 19 are still struggling to find shelter, food, water and other necessities.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled And Abdel Kareem Hana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 46.1 million people were counted in Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — The final results of Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years released Monday show the population has reached 46.1 million. In 2009, an unofficial count estimated the population at 31.6 million.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Most voters want Ukraine to reach settlement with Russia
item.description: Most voters want Ukraine to reach a settlement with Russia as the war between the two countries reaches its three-year anniversary, including a majority of Republicans, Democrats and independents, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: American tourist arrested in Paris after allegedly throwing newborn baby out of hotel window: reports
item.description: Paris authorities arrested an American woman on Monday after she allegedly killed her newborn by hurling the child out of a second-floor window, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Macron project unity despite clear Ukraine-Russia divide
item.description: President Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron sought to project unity during a White House meeting Monday, even as cracks opened further in the alliance between the U.S. and Europe over the path forward in Ukraine. The two met at the Oval Office as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A police union in Haiti demands more protection a day after gangs kill a Kenyan policeman
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Haitian police officers on Monday demanded better protection and treatment a day after gunmen killed a Kenyan policeman who was part of a U.N.-backed mission tasked with fighting gangs in the troubled Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State, Treasury Departments reimposes 'maximum pressure' sanctions on Iran’s oil trade
item.description: The U.S. State and Treasury departments imposed a fresh round of what they called "maximum pressure" sanctions on Iran’s "shadowy" oil trade on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' health shows 'slight improvement' as he has 'resumed work,' Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis' health showed a “slight improvement" Monday as he “resumed work" at the hospital and called a priest at a parish in Gaza, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More pressure on Rwanda as Congo says rebel uprising has killed over 7,000 people this year
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — More than 7,000 people have died this year as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have captured unprecedented amounts of territory in mineral-rich eastern Congo, Congo's prime minister said Monday, as the European Union announced it would review an agreement with Rwanda on critical raw m
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and WILSON MCMAKIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Germany’s new conservative leader looks to ‘achieve independence’ from US
item.description: Germany's incoming chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has said an "absolute priority" for him will be to distance Europe from the U.S. following comments by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany will likely continue its leadership on climate action. But expect a change in tone
item.description: As Germany prepares for a change of government, climate experts are hopeful that Sunday's election results mean that the country will continue to be a leader in ambitious efforts to slash planet-warming emissions and will keep growing its renewable energy industries.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelensky praises ‘absolute heroism of Ukrainians’ three years after Russian invasion
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is praising the “absolute heroism” of his people on the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion. “Three years of resistance. Three years of gratitude. Three years of absolute heroism of Ukrainians. I am proud of Ukraine!” Zelensky posted to X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Surgeon on trial for allegedly raping, abusing 299 victims, mostly child patients
item.description: A former French surgeon accused of raping or sexually abusing 299 victims, mostly children under his care, is set to stand trial in France on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: On third anniversary of Ukraine invasion, European leaders show support, express unease
item.description: European leaders voiced support for Ukraine on the third anniversary of the Russia invasion while demanding a say in any US-Russia peace arrangement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis resting on day 10 in hospital for lung infection that led to early stages of kidney insufficiency
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, was resting Monday morning, his 10th day at the hospital for a complex lung infection that led to the early stages of kidney insufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis remains in critical condition but has shown slight improvement in laboratory tests.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States has split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi lands safely in Rome after security concern
item.description: ROME (AP) — An American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi landed safely in Rome on Sunday afternoon after it was diverted due to a security concern, which later proved to be “non-credible,” the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s military breaks paramilitary group's siege of crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than yearlong siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservative candidate pulls ahead in German election, according to early exit polls
item.description: Center-right opposition leader Friedrich Merz claimed victory in Germany’s national election on Sunday after exit polls showed his bloc in the lead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis shares written message while continuing hospitalization
item.description: Pope Francis purportedly shared a written message from his hospital bed while remaining in critical condition and continuing treatment in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Isolated in 'harsh conditions:' Deportee from US details legal limbo in Panama camp near Darien Gap
item.description: CARTI, Panama (AP) — A deportee from the United States detained in a camp in rural Panama, among a hundred who refused to return to their countries, described on Saturday waiting in limbo under “harsh conditions” and cut off from access to legal council and other rights.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in Hamas captivity
item.description: Edan Alexander spent most of his life in Tenafly, New Jersey, before moving to Israel and enlisting in the IDF. On Oct. 7, 2023, he was taken hostage and is still being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump envoy visits injured Ukrainian soldiers to deliver message, gets asked simple question
item.description: Just days before the three-year anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Trump’s envoy seeks to end the bloody conflict and secure compensation from Ukraine for U.S. military aid to the war-torn nation that is fighting for its survival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Economy, immigration, Elon Musk at center of German election; conservative candidate favored to win
item.description: Germans go to the polls Sunday. Chancellor Scholz's Social Democrats are likely to lose to the conservative CDU party, as the right-wing Alternative for Germany party is likely to make gains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation
